chunks.title: Introduction to the Randle Cycle
chunks.summary: The speaker addresses questions regarding the Randle cycle, particularly concerning its implications for diet, specifically whether combining carbohydrates and fats is detrimental to health. They emphasize the misunderstanding that surrounds this biochemistry concept, especially in the carnivore community.
chunks.transcript: so the next question i want to address is on the randle cycle and this is essentially a question from caitlin she says paul i've been hearing a lot about the randall cycle i don't fully understand the biochemistry does the randall cycle mean that we shouldn't eat carbohydrates and fat together is this going to make me fat if i do this how should i navigate eating fruit on my animal-based diet so i think there's a lot of people in the dogmatic carnivore community chirping about randall cycle randall cycle when i am talking about including fruit on my animal-based diet

chunks.title: Biochemical Mechanism of the Randle Cycle
chunks.summary: The speaker explains the biochemical basis of the Randle cycle, describing substrate level competition in metabolism. They discuss how glucose metabolism inhibits fatty acid oxidation and vice versa, highlighting the importance of metabolic flexibility in human biochemistry.
chunks.transcript: biochemistry underlying the randle cycle this doesn't make a whole lot of sense so let's just start with what the randle cycle is it's essentially substrate level competition that chunks.title: Misconceptions about Weight Gain
chunks.summary: The speaker challenges the belief that combining fruit with animal fats will lead to weight gain, providing personal anecdotes and observations. They underline that many individuals adapting to an animal-based diet often experience weight loss and improved body composition.
chunks.transcript: normal human biochemistry i think most of the time you don't want to be in ketosis you want your muscles to be insulin sensitive you want the glucose disposal you want the glucose disposal to be happening at the level of your muscles as you know if you've listened to my podcast on ketosis why i'm not a fan the recent one with thomas de lauer when you are in ketosis your fasting blood sugar will be higher because you are physiologically insulin resistant your non-esterified fatty acids in the blood will be higher if you listen to my blood work podcast you know that my non-esterified fatty acids are very low right now 0.12 because i am including carbohydrates in my diet when i was on a ketogenic diet my nanocere fatty acids were much higher 0.6 this is physiologic insulin resistance this is essentially what the randall cycle is talking about but i think this gets conflated and misunderstood a lot in these dare i say dogmatic ketogenic dogmatic carnivore communities to say that you will get fat if you eat fruit and animal fat together and i am living proof of that as i am ballooning and getting super fat this is obviously a joke so what we can see very clearly from thousands of people who are eating an animal-based diet we hope that thousands more will join us for animal base 30 at heart and soil that when you eat an animal based diet a lot of people lose weight most people are going to lose weight they're going to become more jacked they're going to become more lean so clearly eating fruit and animal fats together doesn't make you fat that's not

chunks.title: Misinterpretation of the Randall Cycle
chunks.summary: The speaker argues that the Randle cycle has been widely misrepresented in discussions about diet, particularly concerning insulin resistance and fat storage. They clarify that including carbohydrates like fruit does not necessarily lead to weight gain and detail the physiological principles behind these metabolic processes.
